name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #2
description: Florentine Groteschi #2 is a crt goblin single-panel relic built around Mona Lisa and Forum Signature Baroque. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Sette di Denari, used here as the witness card. A touch-grass decree from the garden wall gets pulled into a skibidi audio guide under museum headphones, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. The caption has to tell the truth because the plaque cannot fit all the gossip. The generated insert, a floor-price crown made of unsold listing cards, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks DELULU at Σ 450.

attributes.value: a floor-price crown made of unsold listing cards

chess_piece.name: Rook

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a floor-price crown made of unsold listing cards

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Mona Lisa anchors the historical source. Sette di Denari supplies the witness logic. The floor-price crown made of unsold listing cards acts as the modern meme-object. The crt goblin palette and Forum Signature Baroque styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Mona Lisa is treated as evidence in a laurenziana incident. The old-world clue is a touch-grass decree from the garden wall; the modern-world echo is a skibidi audio guide under museum headphones. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Sette di Denari card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. The caption has to tell the truth because the plaque cannot fit all the gossip. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

slang_for_normal_plebs.translation_for_normal_plebs: did it perfectly; in this collection a fresco, saint, or relic can 'eat' like a viral post

pure absurdist groteschi signal; a choir, relic, or interface acting possessed by meme culture

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.
